Now we're kind of building this attractive character like what distribution channel should I be building? Okay? Like Oprah was probably a good idea for when she was sitting down the first time and be like, hmm, I should be writing newspaper articles. Probably wasn't a good fit for her. She was awesome on TV. So that was the distribution channel that she chose. And one thing that I really struggled with for a long time is that I thought I had to be doing all of these and because of that I wasn't ever good at any of them. And so for most of you guys you're not going to do all of them. I'm going to share five with you and there's, you need to pick two of these five. And one of them has to be email. So good news is I'm going to help you guys email, but email you have to do because that's like, that's like, that's better than prime time TV. It's like the best one. It's the easiest to do. It's, it's, you have to be email. Okay? Now I'm going to liken these to other things that you are familiar with. So email is very similar to direct mail. Right? Back in the day we put letters and envelopes and mail them to people and that's how we get access to them. So direct mail is like email, except for it's, it's a lot cheaper, which is nice. This question, do I recommend direct mail these days? Yeah, definitely. We use it for front and acquisition, but also for, for back and stuff. I bet probably a third of you guys are here, probably from a direct mail campaign that got you to call into our, to the call center. So yeah, definitely use, use both. More so usually on like, if you're going to do direct mail, it's better, like the way to cut your teeth and start it is do it to your existing customers, to upgrade them, to send them up as opposed to doing cold, like to get new customers because it's really expensive and really hard to get it right to get new customers, but it's really easy to your existing customers to make it work. Number two is Facebook. Someone's talking about Drew yesterday. So this is Drew Canole. I don't know what he does like on YouTube and I think he's got a couple channels, but he's awesome on Facebook. If you noticed my fan page, asked me the last time I've done my fan page was, I don't know, it's been months. Like I hate Facebook. I get the sick feeling. You know you log in, you see the little numbers at the top. I have like a thousand plus friend, new friend request. I've got like 80 new messages and all the stuff and I just like, I get sick to my stomach. I'm like, I always, every time I'm on Facebook, I'm like, and then I just turn it down. I can't, it makes me sick to even log in. So for me, I've tried to do this distribution. I try like, everyone's doing Facebook, I gotta do Facebook. I can't do it. Like it's just not for me. Okay? You're that guy or girl who just loves Facebook. Like this might be it, but for me it's not. So Facebook is the equivalent of networking. Go to networking events, hanging out and talking. Anyone ever seen this dude on YouTube? Nobody? DJ has. Okay, so here's the story. So there's this homeless dude. Well, he's not homeless. He's got an RV. So he's got a home. He makes his YouTube videos of him in front of his RV juicing, usually without a shirt on. One video he was dressed like Jesus. I don't know. He's really weird, right? But this dude loves juicing. Super passionate. Every single day he's making juicing videos of him out in front of his RV, doing stuff, right? And he's got a huge following. 300,000 people saw this video. Everyone's videos gets, you know, a quarter million or more views. And it's one day our buddy, Garrett, is like obsessed with this guy. This guy's awesome. He's like the best juicing guy in the world. So Garrett emails him and was like, hey man, can I call you on the phone and let me like pick your brain on juicing? And the guy's like, yeah, you can. Here's a PayPal order button. Send me 500 bucks and we'll talk for an hour. This dude who's homeless is charging 500 bucks an hour for juicing advice. Does that help anybody understand that like the power of a platform? Okay? It's because he's out there. He's attractive, cared all the stuff's out there and people are following him. He's got fans. Now he's not famous. I know most of you guys never heard of him, but he's internet famous. Okay, I was at church the other day. And at church I try to like not tell people what I do because it's really hard when people want to ask you all sorts of questions. So I'm pretty like, I don't talk about it. I'm kind of quiet. I'm really shy person in my real life. Everyone's muscle man finds out and they come to me and they're like, hey, I saw your video man. I didn't know you were famous. And I'm like, no, no, I'm internet famous. Big difference. Internet famous means none of you guys know who I am. Okay? And that's the key. Like you guys want to become internet famous. You don't want to go be like real famous because that's not fun. But internet famous is awesome because there's a segment of people who know you like you and love you and will pay you 500 bucks an hour to teach them how to juice stuff. You like juicing. Your juicing mate is awesome. Your drink is better than that. 500 bucks an hour. Doctors don't charge that much, do they? Maybe they do. I don't know. But that's the power of this stuff, you guys. You build this attractive character. It's interesting. People don't look at you like another person. Even though you're doing this stuff with the back story, all these things are trying to relate to their level. They still look at you at a different level. Okay? They look at this guy at a different level because he's built the distribution channel. So for him, YouTube's his channel. Okay? So yeah, think about it. If that's the one that resonates most with you, that one's the equivalent of TV. You guys look at it. So I just bought a brand new 60-inch TV. We plugged it in. And I couldn't figure out how to get to the real channels. Like, it was really hard to get to the real channels. But there's all these little apps. And you click on the app and it takes you to YouTube and you subscribe. And everyone I've ever subscribed to YouTube channel is right there. So it was easier for me to find people I had subscribed to on YouTube than it was for me to find like Fox TV. I couldn't get to my news channels. And that's the future where things are going right now. This is the big shift. It's made it so all of us can become super rich that we couldn't before. Okay? Before we had to get on TV and we had to do all this stuff to become famous. Now you just make a YouTube channel. You get some following. People go subscribe to you. They sit down at night. They turn on the TV and they watch you on YouTube. Is that weird? It's not like it used to be where if you went on your computer and you find this person, like people subscribed to you now and then they subscribe to that to their TV and you become late night TV form. That's the place we're going. You start focusing now. I've been telling people this for 10 years. But if you start focusing now on YouTube's your channel, start focusing on growing that in a year from now, five years from now, 10 years from now, you have a million viewers. Guess what you've got? You've got more viewers than any local station in the world. People are going to be coming begging you to buy ads from you. You own distribution. So I would make it if YouTube is your channel, that's the platform you want to pick. Just focus on that and grow and grow your following because that following will be worth so much money. Cable networks are becoming buying you guys for millions of dollars. It's happening right now. People are getting bought out because of their followings. It's happening right now. It's going to happen more and more as it's harder and harder for all these cable shows and all these stations to compete. Because right now I can get the exact content I want from the dude who I want to hear from for free where I can't get this juicing guy on TV. Next are podcasts. I just started getting into podcasting. It's really fun. Anyone here listen to the marketing in your car podcast? Yeah. So this is my, this is my new channel, one of my new channels. This is my favorite. I tried to podcasting a couple of times. It's really hard because typically most of them make like an hour long show and I can never do that. So I started my very first one where it's just while I'm driving into the office, five minutes a day I just record my thoughts of the day like, oh, this is something cool. We just did. It worked awesome. So if you're not listening to it, you probably should. Like all my best ideas, like I literally share them all to my podcast. And it's free. You just go to marketingyourcar.com, click on subscribe and then it shows up on your phone. And so what's happening is everyone subscribes that thing. It's like my own radio channel, my own radio station. I can send messages to everyone who's listening whenever I want. And it's the coolest thing in the world. And then what's really interesting is I got some friends who are killing their podcast. They got way more people listening to me. But it's literally like a radio show. One of my buddies, he does an episode every single day and it's 30 minutes long. He has a sponsorship ad at the beginning of the podcast, one in the middle and one at the end. He gets paid per CPM. So per 1,000 users. He gets paid some for each of these companies right now. He gets, I don't know, 100,000 people listening to each one, something like that. And each company, this company does the front one, pays them, I think like $300 or $400 an episode. The middle one pays them like $200, the last one pays them like $500. So he makes $1,200 in ads every time he does a podcast. How often do you think he does a podcast? Seven days a week. He says, Russell, if I don't do a podcast, I'll use $1,200. I don't know if it's anything. I just have to do it. And then I push upload and I make $1,200. Okay? I say, I want to do that. I want to do that three times a day, four times a day. Okay? But that's literally what's happening, guys, is you're creating your own radio station. Can you think about Rush Limbaugh? Think about all these people. I think, I'm not a big radio guy, but all the big radio people, they've got these distribution channels, right? They're getting smaller. They're shrinking because where are people going? They're going to podcasts, subscribing to what they actually want to hear and niche specific stuff they want. And if that's you, you start focusing today, tomorrow, and within a year from now, two years from now, five years from now, you've got a huge following of people. You can first off sell them anything you want down your distribution channel. You can sell ads. You can do sponsor. There's so many things you can do when you have distribution. Okay? And the way people are going to listen to you gets all the attractive character stuff. Because they're going to give you a shot. If they like your thing, they're going to keep listening forever. Okay? So the podcast is equivalent to radio. And then blogging is like newspapers. All this media that we're doing, you guys, online, it's all stuff that happened offline. We couldn't do that before, but nowadays we can. Yeah. So this question was, like if I'm making a YouTube video, I don't want to just send YouTube like, how else can I do it? What I'm saying is, is there's different platforms, right? Email again, we're all in email. But networking, some of you guys are great networkers. That's going to be your thing. Some of you guys are great on video. That should be your core. Some of you guys are great radio. Should be podcasting. Some of you guys are great writers. Should be blog. And they're all going to cross promo. But yeah, there should be one that's like, this is my core. This is my baby. Because as soon as you're doing two things or three things, and it's hard to wake everybody to do a show and write a blog post and do a part. Like, I've tried. I tried. We had teams of people trying to like, keep the system down. We were hitting all the channels and finally I came to the realization. I'm just like, you know what? Everyone who's crushing it, they're crushing it on one thing. And they may add in the second one later. But usually they focus on one first. So let's say it's like Drew Canole, right? He builds up his Facebook to a million people. Now for him to come back and say, hey, you know what? I got a million people here. I'm going to add a YouTube channel. And he adds it and boom, 50% of people come over here. Now he can do two a lot easier. But if you're trying to grow and do like two or three at a time, it's never going to work. So to begin with today, pick one, focus on that. And somewhere down the line you can expand and try to do more. But right now, just try to do one. Julie? Ooh, that's a good question. So is it more like what you want to do more of your audience? I would say more what you want to do. Because otherwise you're not going to. I mean, there's a double-edged sword. You're trying to trick me. No, but because you're right, your audience needs to be there. But at the same time, if it's videos and you hate doing videos, you're never going to do them. And you've got it. I mean, this is, it's got to be something you're passionate about. You wake up in the morning. When I do marketing in your car, wake up, turn on my phone, I start driving. I'm passionate about that. For me to sit down and write a blog post, I literally will sit down. I'll look at that thing for like three hours. Like, huh. Crap. So it's like, what's the channel that you thrive in the best? And I think that you can get your, I think, my guess is that there's people in every one of these channels in whatever space you're in. Some there may be more prevalent. But I would focus on what you like the best or else you're not going to do it. That's a big key. All right. So that's kind of the attractive character stuff, you guys. If I could say one thing of everything you're going to do that's going to be the amplifier for your business, it's like the raw, you bring out like your raw passion as much as possible. Like that's, I look at you guys like Jake Hill or like Gary Vaynerchuk. I look at everyone who I know who's successful and is internet famous, who's growing businesses in any space. They're all hyper, super passionate and excited about the thing and they're just sharing and they're giving and they're trying to do as much as they can. Because of that giving action, all the stuff, people start being attracted and start coming back to you. And it's just, it works. It works every single time. I think it's the foundation for everything else we're going to be talking about. Yeah.
